event_blocks.summary: Savannah XYZ, in an orange suit with a pink visor, initiates a game segment by announcing the intention to 'shove Alejandro' into an inverse teleporter within a vibrant, cartoonish outdoor environment. After a male voice exclaims 'I love public transportation!' and other male voices confirm readiness with 'Okay, you ready?' and 'Let's go!', a device activates with a distinct sound. The scene abruptly shifts to a darker, industrial setting featuring a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus. A character in an orange suit with a green visor screams 'Run! Run! Run away! Leave me!' amidst chaotic sounds and an explosion. The character with the blue visor falls, and a performance report appears, listing all players as 'DECEASED' with a 'GRADE: F'. A detached male voice questions 'Oh, what just happened? I uh, got back from the bathroom,' while Savannah XYZ asks, 'You guys want to play again?'. The environment then transitions to a desaturated, sepia-toned post-disaster scenario. Savannah XYZ, in first-person view with a flashlight, explores a ruined area, strategizing to split into 'team Alejandro' and 'team B,' reflected in a team interface with cartoon avatars. Alejandro jokes 'I go team die. Let's go die,' and later expresses concern about being 'team also die.' Savannah XYZ asks 'Are you ready, Alejandro?', and another male voice questions if Alejandro is 'coming with you or is he still outside?'. The player navigates a dimly lit industrial interior where Alejandro notices a 'glowing yellow apparatus.' Sounds of a device powering up and a metallic clang are heard, followed by a male voice saying 'I got it.' A 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' overlay appears, accompanied by visible health and weight indicators, and a male voice expresses apprehension: 'I hope we didn't do anything stupid by doing this.' Later, in a dark, industrial environment, a red 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' overlay appears as an alarmed female voice exclaims, 'Oh no. Oh no. Radiation.' The player encounters a character in an orange suit with a green visor reacting with alarm near a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus, prompting a female voice to ask, 'So, what do we do with that now?' and suggest, 'Okay, let's just leave that.' A male voice states, 'It's not possible,' before another male voice reveals, 'So you may have noticed we found... we found... Big water fucking factory,' referring to the apparatus. The scene shifts to a red 'Performance Report Interface' displaying 'DECEASED' status for players like Alejandro with a 'GRADE: F' and notes like 'The laziest employee'; a male voice confirms, 'Oh, yeah, she's dead. I don't know why, but she's dead,' while a female voice adds, 'But didn't he say 'lazy'? Well, they died,' with another male voice commenting, 'Anything to get out of a day of work.' The setting transitions to a brighter, enclosed control room with metallic walls, control panels, and bunk beds, where three characters in orange suits are gathered around a console; a male voice asks, 'Do you guys get any loot?' and a female voice responds, 'Yeah, we got the apparatus. What do you mean?'. The player interacts with a terminal displaying planetary data ('HAZARD LEVEL: C,' 'POPULATION: Abandoned,' 'FAUNA: A competitive ecosystem supports aggressive lifeforms') as a male voice discusses mission strategy: 'So, I'll tell you guys about the strat, I learn. We only sell the minimum amount. The more stuff you sell, the higher the next quota is,' explaining, 'We do too good of a job, they like give us more assignments.' As the player moves toward a circular hatch, text overlays 'wait' and 'when i picked it up' coincide with a male voice realizing, 'Wait, wait, wait. I don't have it anymore. I think I threw it away when I picked it up,' while a female voice offers reassurance, 'Don't worry, we got you.'

Character in orange suit with pink visor.description: Wears an orange spacesuit with black straps, a pink visor, and has a cartoonish, expressive face; appears distressed in the control room, leaning over a console.

Team Avatars Interface.description: Cartoon-style avatars labeled 'team alejandro' and 'team b', with one avatar marked 'DECEASED' in red text. The team names are explicitly mentioned by Savannah XYZ during strategy ('I get to be on team Alejandro, and you guys will be team B').

event_blocks.summary: From a first-person perspective in a foggy, desaturated outdoor environment, a character in an orange suit with a pink visor stands near a broken bridge, as HUD elements display health, weight, and the time (8:16 AM). A companion questions the map's scale and the bridge's integrity before the scene shifts to a character in an orange suit with a blue visor examining a cookie mold pan labeled 'Value: 25', prompting an excited "Cookies! Let's go! We are so back!". The player then picks up a large axle, indicated by 'HANDS FULL', and carries it through a dimly lit industrial area, with the companion suggesting using an alternative bridge due to the first being rickety. As the time advances to 6:36 PM, the player enters a tense control room where a character in an orange suit with a black visor appears distressed near a console, and a character in an orange suit with a green visor reacts alarm near a yellow-and-black striped apparatus. Urgent calls of "Alejandro, run! Run!" are heard, followed by speculation about a bridge's structural integrity under duress. Navigating the control room, featuring bunk beds and monitors, the companion notes the bridge is not breaking, but then exclaims "Oh god! No! Don't look back, Savannah!" amidst upbeat, chaotic electronic music and a 'Radiation Warning Overlay' indicating 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv'. The sequence concludes with the player interacting with a Planetary Data Terminal showing 'HAZARD LEVEL: C', 'POPULATION: Abandoned', and 'FAUNA: A competitive ecosystem supports aggressive lifeforms', a 'NEW FOLLOWER' notification for 'MELOKOMUPPET', and a deep voice stating, "You're smart now, you fucked up."

raw_segments: The video opens with a first-person perspective in a foggy, desaturated outdoor environment, showing a character in an orange suit with a pink visor standing near a broken bridge. As the player, accompanied by a companion, approaches a wooden bridge, the companion's voice asks, "Oh wait, is this map? This is the big boy map. I don't like it here. Should we go down?" and then, "What is going on with the bridge?", as on-screen HUD elements display a health bar, weight indicator, and the time (8:16 AM). The scene shifts to a close-up of a character in an orange suit with a blue visor, holding a clipboard, interacting with a cookie mold pan labeled 'Value: 25', prompting the companion to exclaim "Cookies!" and "Let's go! We are so back!" The player then picks up a large axle, indicated by the 'HANDS FULL' status, and carries it through a dimly lit industrial area, while the companion suggests, "Do we want to carry this stuff back and see what it is? ... I'm going to use the other bridge because that one's rickety." As the time progresses to 6:36 PM, the player enters a tense control room, where a character in an orange suit with a black visor appears distressed near a console, and a character in an orange suit with a green visor reacts with alarm near a yellow-and-black striped apparatus. The companion's voice expresses urgency, saying "Oh no! Oh no! Alejandro, run! Run!" and later, "I don't know if that thing can use this type of bridge. Let's test if this guy comes, will it break? I hope it does." The player navigates the control room, which features bunk beds and monitors, as the companion notes, "It's not breaking. Let's go! It's not breaking. Oh god! No! Don't look back, Savannah!" An upbeat, slightly chaotic electronic music track underscores the tension, while a 'Radiation Warning Overlay' appears, indicating 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv'. The sequence concludes with the player interacting with a Planetary Data Terminal displaying 'HAZARD LEVEL: C', 'POPULATION: Abandoned', and 'FAUNA: A competitive ecosystem supports aggressive lifeforms', while a 'NEW FOLLOWER' notification for 'MELOKOMUPPET' appears, culminating in a distinct, deeper voice stating, "You're smart now, you fucked up."

The video opens from a first-person perspective in a dimly lit, industrial control room, displaying a console with multiple screens and a health/weight HUD, as confident voices declare, "This game is so easy" and "It's in the bag." The player, holding a flashlight, navigates a dark corridor where a character in an orange suit with a black visor is seen. The environment quickly shifts to a more hazardous area, marked by a radiation warning overlay indicating 70 mSv, prompting a voice to exclaim, "I'm terrified now, I think." The player then encounters a character in an orange suit with a green visor, who appears visibly alarmed near a yellow-and-black striped apparatus. The scene transitions to a brighter control room where a character in an orange suit with a pink visor is distressed, leaning over a console, while another in an orange suit with a blue visor holds a clipboard. Amidst this, a voice attempts communication, stating, "Roger, this is a Roger over. I am wasting the walkie-talkie battery, over, over." The player interacts with a Planetary Data Terminal displaying 'HAZARD LEVEL: C' and 'POPULATION: Abandoned', providing grim context. Subsequently, the player monitors a screen showing a map with a teddy bear icon and coordinates, holding a walkie-talkie, as panicked voices are heard, questioning "Wait, what?" and exclaiming "Oh my god!" and "We gotta head back!" The player switches camera views, with on-screen text indicating 'MONITORING: Wolf' and a red overlay warning of increased radiation, while voices desperately note, "We got a bolt, that's all we got." A brief moment of relief with "Hello, we're alive!" is quickly overshadowed by growing doubt, with a voice admitting, "I do not know if this is in the bag." The sequence concludes with a performance report interface displaying 'DECEASED' status for multiple players and a 'GRADE: F', confirming the mission's ultimate failure.

event_blocks.summary: From a first-person perspective within a dimly lit industrial control room, the player, holding a walkie-talkie, expresses feelings of distress while monitoring surveillance feeds of team members like 'Alejandro', 'Savannah', 'Scorpius', and 'Wolf' on multiple screens, alongside a green-tinted map. A panicked female voice announces the presence of a large monster, described as a 'big zombie boy.' The player navigates the control room, observing a distressed character in an orange suit with a black visor near a console, amidst a male voice crying out, 'It's off to me!' followed by a monster's growl and screams. This orange-suited character is later seen near a yellow-and-black striped apparatus, reacting with alarm as the female voice shouts, 'No, the monster!' After a loud thud and the female voice's concerned 'Oh no,' she informs Alejandro that he appears to be alone. Alejandro's cryptic response is interrupted by a warning that someone is approaching him, prompting him to state he has nowhere to go. The player, via walkie-talkie, reassures Alejandro they are en route. The female voice urgently warns Alejandro of a threat behind him, then tells him it's time to leave, apologizing to 'John.' The player then navigates a dark, hazardous area using thermal vision, noting a '70 mSv' radiation warning, and interacts with a control panel, pressing a red 'Start ship' button. The segment concludes with a 'Base44' advertisement showcasing a laptop screen and a voiceover promoting an AI building capability.

raw_segments: The video opens with a first-person view inside a dimly lit, industrial control room, where the player, holding a walkie-talkie, states, "I am going insane. But I hope you guys are having a good time." Multiple monitors display surveillance feeds of team members like 'Alejandro', 'Savannah', 'Scorpius', and 'Wolf', alongside a green-tinted map. A female voice then urgently exclaims, "I hear a monster! It's a big zombie boy. It's the big boy, you guys." The player moves through the control room, encountering a character in an orange suit with a black visor who appears distressed, leaning over a console, and a male voice cries, "It's off to me!" followed by a monster's growl and frantic screams. This character is later seen standing near a yellow-and-black striped apparatus, reacting with alarm as the female voice shouts, "No, the monster!" After a loud thud, the female voice expresses concern, "Oh no," then addresses a team member, "Alejandro, I'm gonna be real with you, I think it's just you out there." Alejandro responds, "Well, you know what they say," before the female voice warns, "Someone's coming towards you, run!" Alejandro replies, "Uh, I have nowhere to go now." The player, possibly speaking through the walkie-talkie, reassures, "Alright, I'm on my way. Don't you worry about it." The female voice frantically warns, "Behind you, behind you! Oh no," followed by another monster growl. She then tells Alejandro, "I think it's time to go. I'm sorry, John." The player navigates a dark, hazardous area in thermal vision, revealing a '70 mSv' radiation warning, before interacting with a control panel to press a red button labeled 'Start ship'. The segment concludes with a promotional ad for 'Base44', featuring a laptop screen and a voiceover stating, "Imagine being able to build anything, just by thinking about it. Meet Base 44. A complete AI..."

event_blocks.summary: Following a desaturated view of a table with a walkie-talkie and cookie mold pan, the scene shifts to a vibrant, outdoor Martian-like environment where three characters in orange spacesuits with pink, green, and black visors stand near a base structure. The pink visor character expresses relief and a need for therapy, while the black visor character wishes good luck and the green visor character admits to being scared. The pink visor character confirms possession of a walkie-talkie, which the black visor character also claims. From a first-person perspective, the player, equipped with a walkie-talkie and displaying HUD elements, navigates rocky terrain. The green visor character humorously offers their personality, and the pink visor character suggests the player take a flashlight. During navigation, the green visor character advises taking an 'L' to survive, prompting a playful retort from the pink visor character and a stern warning from the black visor character. The black visor character makes a jest about the player's perceived Britishness, and the green visor character warns about hitting the ceiling while attempting vertical movement. Transitioning into a dimly lit industrial corridor, the green visor character is seen falling or being thrown. The pink visor character notes the convenience of not having to walk back to the ship, a statement the black visor character humorously labels as destructive. The player discovers the deceased body of 'savannahXYZ', marked with a yellow emoji avatar. The green visor character suggests collecting their own body, which the black visor character acknowledges. The green visor character then urgently questions if a 'red thing' is present, immediately preceding a loud explosion. A red light and radiation warning overlay appear, indicating the death of Alejandro. The pink visor character observes the threat is 'in the wall,' and the black visor character calls out. The green visor character questions if they died by the same cause, and the pink visor character laments seeing the word 'landmine' too slowly. The player reflects on the situation, comparing it to reality show interviews, as the green visor character comments on the perceived value of something. The scene concludes with the pink visor character sitting distressed in a chair, the player's walkie-talkie showing deceased teammate statuses, and the black visor character musing about post-event interviews.

Control Room Environment.description: A brightly lit, enclosed space with metallic walls, control panels, bunk beds, and a central console; features multiple monitors, a walkie-talkie, and a yellow-and-black striped apparatus, suggesting a spaceship or research facility interior.

Character in orange suit with pink visor.description: Wears an orange spacesuit with black straps and a pink visor; appears in the outdoor scene and later seated in a chair, looking distressed and reflective. She has a female voice, expressing relief and humor ('Wow, great job you guys. Now I need therapy.'), confirming she has the walkie-talkie, playfully stating 'You're not my dad,' expressing relief about not walking back to the ship, observing 'It's in the wall,' and lamenting her slow reaction to danger ('I saw the words landmine way too slowly.').

Walkie-Talkie.description: A handheld communication device with a digital screen showing time and channel; held by the player throughout the segment, used for team communication and displaying status updates for deceased teammates. It is explicitly mentioned by the pink visor character ('I've got the walkie-talkie') and the black visor character ('I have walkie-talkie').

Team Avatars Interface.description: Cartoon-style avatars labeled 'savannahXYZ (Dead)' and 'Alejandro (Dead)' with red text and speaker icons, indicating deceased teammates and their status during gameplay.

Yellow-and-Black Striped Apparatus.description: A large, suspended device with yellow and black stripes, possibly a bomb or hazardous object, located in the industrial setting. The green visor character asks 'Is that a red thing?' just before an explosion, and the pink visor character says 'It's in the wall,' possibly referring to this or a related hazard, indicating its dangerous nature.

Deceased Teammate Bodies.description: The physical remains of teammates 'savannahXYZ' and 'Alejandro', shown lying on the ground in the industrial corridor, marked with red text and avatars, indicating their death and the player's discovery of them.

Radiation Warning Overlay.description: A red triangular warning sign with text 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' appearing during exploration of the dark industrial area, indicating environmental danger.

First-Person HUD Elements.description: On-screen interface including health bar, weight indicator, flashlight controls, and time display (e.g., '9:02 AM'); also shows 'Drop Walkie-talkie : [G]', 'Power button : [Q]', and 'Send voice : [LMB]'.

Character in orange suit with green viso.description: Wears an orange suit with a green visor; initially seen standing outdoors, later shown falling or being thrown in the industrial corridor, and marked as deceased. He has a male voice, expressing fear ('I've never been so scared in my life'), giving advice ('You gotta take the L, otherwise you won't make it'), warning to 'Try to hit the vertical careful, you get the ceiling,' suggesting 'You might have to collect mine,' urgently asking 'Is that a red thing?' before an explosion, questioning 'We died by the same thing, right?', and remarking 'It just looked valuable.'

Character in orange suit with black viso.description: Wears an orange spacesuit with black straps and a black visor; seen from behind in the first-person view, navigating the environment and interacting with the walkie-talkie. He has a male voice, offering a hopeful 'Good luck, I'll see you back here alive hopefully,' confirming 'I have walkie-talkie,' sternly stating 'Don't fucking tell me that again, can't watch you,' jokingly commenting 'Don't be so British,' humorously labeling a comment 'the most destructive thing you've ever spoken,' calling out 'Buddy?', and reflecting on the situation by comparing it to 'reality shows where there are interviews after something happens?'

Industrial Corridor Environment.description: A dimly lit, metallic corridor with grated floors, pipes, and structural beams, creating a claustrophobic and hazardous atmosphere; features red lights and warning signs, indicating danger and decay.

raw_segments: The video opens with a desaturated, top-down view of a table scattered with items including a cookie mold pan and a walkie-talkie, accompanied by the on-screen text '[Wait for ship to land]' and a gentle, ethereal background music with a metallic clink. The scene transitions to a vibrant, outdoor Martian-like environment where three characters in orange spacesuits with distinct visors (pink, green, and black) stand near a base structure. The character with the pink visor, a female voice, expresses relief and humor, stating, "Wow, great job you guys. Now I need therapy." A male voice (black visor) offers a hopeful "Good luck, I'll see you back here alive hopefully," while another male voice (green visor) admits, "I've never been so scared in my life." The pink visor character confirms, "Alright, so I've got the walkie-talkie, you guys have stuff too?" to which the black visor character replies, "I have walkie-talkie." From a first-person perspective, the player holds a walkie-talkie, with the HUD displaying health, weight, and flashlight controls. The green visor character humorously states, "I have my personality and...", before the pink visor character interjects, "Alright, why don't you take the flashlight?" The player navigates rocky, debris-strewn terrain, communicating with teammates via the walkie-talkie. The green visor character advises, "You gotta take the L, otherwise you won't make it," which is met with a playful "You're not my dad" from the pink visor character, followed by a stern "Don't fucking tell me that again, can't watch you" from the black visor character. The player continues, with the black visor character jokingly commenting, "Don't be so British," and the green visor character warning, "Try to hit the vertical careful, you get the ceiling." As the player moves through a dimly lit industrial corridor, the character with the green visor is seen falling or being thrown. The pink visor character expresses relief, "The good part of this is I do not have to walk back to the ship," which the black visor character humorously labels "the most destructive thing you've ever spoken." The player discovers the body of 'savannahXYZ' (Dead), marked with a yellow emoji avatar. The green visor character suggests, "You might have to collect mine," followed by the black visor character's "I see..." The green visor character then urgently asks, "You might want to stop. Is that a red thing?" just before a loud explosion sound. A red light and a radiation warning overlay appear, revealing that another teammate, Alejandro, is also deceased. The pink visor character observes, "It's in the wall," while the black visor character calls out, "Buddy?" The green visor character questions, "We died by the same thing, right?" and the pink visor character laments, "I saw the words landmine way too slowly." The player reflects on the situation, comparing it to reality show interviews, as the green visor character remarks, "It just looked valuable." The scene ends with the character in the pink visor sitting in a chair, appearing distressed, as the player’s walkie-talkie displays the status of deceased teammates, while the black visor character muses, "Is this like, you know, in those reality shows where there are interviews after something happens?"

event_blocks.summary: In a post-mission debriefing, characters in orange suits express regret over recent deaths, with one mentioning calling a teammate 'British' before their demise and another lamenting an inability to see a teammate's location on the map, attributing their deaths to landmines and a "funny coincidence." A 'Performance Report' interface confirms 'Alejandro', 'savannahXYZ', and 'Wolf' as deceased, 'Scorpius' as alive, and the mission as a failure with a 'GRADE: F'. The perspective shifts to first-person within a dusty, industrial control room, where the player, in an orange suit with a black visor and holding a walkie-talkie, panics amidst static and beeping, declaring innocence. Navigating the environment, the player encounters a yellow-and-black striped apparatus and later discovers the bodies of Alejandro and Wolf in a metallic corridor. The player attempts to distract swarming bees with money, resulting in an attack and the loss of $53, with a suggestion to distract the bees for profit. Urgent shouts of "Get in the car!" follow. A 'Fines' screen displays a $53 penalty for one casualty and zero bodies recovered. The player tests a yellow-handled shovel, confirming its effectiveness on others, before encountering a 'portal turret' which is subsequently destroyed. Deceased teammates 'Alejandro' and 'savannahXYZ' are found, but looting is hindered by a 'HANDS FULL' status due to a picked-up large axle. The player reacts with alarm to a suspended yellow-and-black striped apparatus. Amidst red warning lights, the player drops a body, identified as 'Yasha', and panics about losing 'bees' and suffering heavy losses, with the HUD displaying 'LIFE SUPPORT: OFFLINE'. The player character, speaking with a distorted, panicked voice, expresses distress over losing 'bees' and heavy team losses, while the Team Avatars Interface shows 'Scorpius (Dead)' and 'savannahXYZ (Dead)'. A teammate admits to not understanding the situation, and the player frantically asks how to use equipment, lacking a license. The player questions if the shovel works on people, receiving confirmation that it has been tested. A large yellow-and-black striped apparatus causes alarm, prompting shouts of "Get out!". A distorted radio voice warns the facility is unsafe, contradicted by another voice. A sharp metallic clang is heard, followed by cries of "No! Oh, they dragged them away!". Descending a metal staircase, the player discovers the bodies of Alejandro and savannahXYZ, vowing revenge. The HUD displays 'HANDS FULL' after picking up a large axle, and the character loots a body with a shovel. A male voice repeatedly says "No, no, no, no, no" as a scraping sound is heard, followed by an exclamation, "Proving excuse!". An invention to support a teammate named Tom is discussed, with the decision to keep him as part of the team. Upbeat, retro-futuristic music plays as the player, 'Fixer', communicates via walkie-talkie with a distressed 'Breaker'. A loud impact or explosion sound follows. The player states, "I could take her." Muffled voices are heard, with one remarking, "It could be worse." An authoritative voice suggests splitting up. The player navigates a dark, narrow industrial corridor with red warning lights and a 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' overlay. Deceased teammates 'Alejandro' and 'Wolf' are encountered, and a teammate dismisses the shovel, siding with 'Yasha'. The player questions "Wait, where's Yasha?" and "Yasha?" A plea for a "mental safe" is made, followed by gasps. A fire exit is chosen over returning. A "bracket" and a "battery" are mentioned. A loud, mechanical whirring/rumbling sound occurs as the player interacts with a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus, causing alarm. A male voice questions, "What's the worst thing that could get us?" followed by another loud impact/explosion and a startled "Oh!". The sequence ends with the player approaching a door labeled 'Scorpius,' with walkie-talkie controls displayed.

Control Room Environment.description: A brightly lit, enclosed space with metallic walls, control panels, bunk beds, and a central console; features multiple monitors, a walkie-talkie, and a yellow-and-black striped apparatus, suggesting a spaceship or research facility interior.

Character in orange suit with pink visor.description: Wears an orange spacesuit with black straps and a pink visor; appears seated in a chair, looking distressed and reflective during the debriefing scene, and is heard communicating via walkie-talkie, stating "Fixer, I'm broken, over."

Performance Report Interface.description: A red overlay displaying player names (Alejandro, savannahXYZ, Scorpius, Wolf) with 'DECEASED' status and a 'GRADE: F' at the end of the sequence, indicating failure or death.

Walkie-Talkie.description: A handheld communication device used by the player ("Fixer") to initiate calls ("Breaker, this is Fixer") and receive distressed responses ("Fixer, I'm broken, over") from teammates, displaying status updates like 'Drop Walkie-talkie : [G]' and 'Send voice : [LMB]'.

Character in orange suit with blue visor.description: Wears an orange suit with a blue visor and holds a clipboard; appears seated in a chair during the debriefing. While not explicitly identified by voice, an authoritative male voice suggesting, "Okay, let's split up now," could potentially be this character.

Yellow-and-Black Striped Apparatus.description: A large, suspended device with yellow and black stripes, possibly a bomb or hazardous object, located in the industrial setting; visually causes alarm and is audibly associated with a loud, mechanical whirring/rumbling sound and sudden impacts/explosions.

Cookie Mold Pan.description: A metallic pan with a cookie-shaped mold, labeled 'Value: 25', interacted with by the character in the orange suit with a blue visor.

Radiation Warning Overlay.description: A red triangular warning sign with text 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' appearing during exploration of the dark industrial area, indicating environmental danger.

First-Person HUD Elements.description: On-screen interface including health bar, weight indicator, flashlight controls, and time display; also shows 'Drop Walkie-talkie : [G]', 'Power button : [Q]', and 'Send voice : [LMB]'.

Industrial Corridor Environment.description: A dimly lit, metallic corridor with grated floors, pipes, and structural beams, creating a claustrophobic and hazardous atmosphere; features red lights and warning signs, indicating danger and decay, and is the setting for discussions about "fire exit" and the source of loud impacts and mechanical whirring.

Large Axle.description: A heavy, cylindrical metal object picked up by the player, causing the 'HANDS FULL' status to appear on the HUD, preventing further looting.

Team Avatars Interface.description: Cartoon-style avatars labeled 'Alejandro (Dead)' and 'Wolf (Dead)' with red text and speaker icons, indicating deceased teammates and their status during gameplay. The dialogue also mentions 'Savannah' and 'Yasha' as teammates.

Monitoring Screen Interface.description: A green-tinted screen displaying a map with coordinates, icons (including a teddy bear), and the text 'MONITORING: Wolf'. This screen visually tracks 'Wolf', whose death is confirmed and discussed by teammates. It also displays 'PROFIT QUOTA: 10 / 1253' and 'DEADLINE: 2 Days'.

Planetary Data Terminal.description: A screen displaying information such as 'HAZARD LEVEL: C', 'POPULATION: Abandoned', and 'FAUNA: A competitive ecosystem supports aggressive lifeforms', providing context for the mission or environment, aligning with audio discussions of dangerous creatures and deceased teammates.

Deceased Teammate Bodies.description: The physical remains of teammates 'Alejandro' and 'Wolf,' shown lying on the ground in the industrial corridor, marked with red text and avatars. Another teammate, 'Savannah,' is mentioned in dialogue ("Uh, yeah, Savannah, I guess"), implying her status as a team member, possibly deceased.

Bees.description: Swarming insects that attack the characters; appear in the industrial setting, causing panic and distraction, and are referenced in the dialogue as being ordered for $100, with a plan to 'distract the bees'.

Base44 Advertisement.description: A promotional ad featuring a laptop screen with the tagline 'Let’s make your dream a reality. Right now.' and a 'Start now' button; includes a website URL 'base44.com' and a logo with an orange circle and horizontal lines.

Character in orange suit with black viso.description: Wears an orange spacesuit with black straps and a black visor; seen from a first-person perspective, holding a flashlight and walkie-talkie. Audibly identifies as "Fixer" ("Breaker, this is Fixer"), states "I could take her," mentions "Savannah," and asks "Wait, where's Yasha?" and "Yasha?". This character also utters the initial "No, no, no, no, no" and a startled "Oh!" at the end.

Shovel.description: A yellow-handled shovel tested by the player and later looted; audibly mentioned by a female voice who says, "I've had enough of you and your shovel."

Life Support System.description: An in-game system indicated by the on-screen text '[LIFE SUPPORT: OFFLINE]', signaling a critical failure that coincides with the player's frantic shouts about losing 'so much'.

Fines Screen.description: A purple overlay displaying 'Fines' with text '1 casualty: -20% (0 bodies recovered)' and 'DUE: $53,' visually confirming the penalty, which is also mentioned by a player who states, "We lost $53."

Turret.description: A white, robotic turret with a red eye, identified by the player as a "fucking portal turret" and later confirmed to be "dead" after sounds of gunfire and lasers.

Rubber ducky.description: A small, yellow rubber duck found on the floor, labeled 'Value: 77', picked up by the player and held in hand, adding a surreal or humorous element to the tense environment.

raw_segments: The video opens in a post-mission debriefing where two distressed characters in orange suits sit, their voices echoing with regret. The character with the pink visor confesses, "I will be honest, I called him British moments before I died, and I can't help but wonder if those two things are connected." The character with the blue visor, holding a clipboard, adds, "So I don't know what got Alejandro. I couldn't see anything on the map for him," before revealing, "We both died to landmines, and I think it's still a little bit of a funny coincidence." A 'Performance Report' overlay then appears, marking 'Alejandro', 'savannahXYZ', and 'Wolf' as deceased, 'Scorpius' as alive, and the mission with a failing 'GRADE: F'. The scene abruptly shifts to a first-person perspective inside a control room, where the player, wearing an orange suit with a black visor and holding a walkie-talkie, panics amidst static and beeping, shouting, "I'M INNOCENT!" as they navigate a dusty, industrial environment. The player encounters a yellow-and-black striped apparatus, reacting with alarm, and later discovers the bodies of Alejandro and Wolf in a metallic corridor, confirming their deaths. Amidst the tense, claustrophobic atmosphere, the player attempts to distract swarming bees with money, only to be attacked, leading to the realization that the team has lost $53, with a voice suggesting, "So that hive they have is worth 100 bucks. So maybe I could distract the bees while I quickly go grab it, then it'll go towards me." Another voice remarks, "You look like you had it in the bag." The player then reacts to a sudden event with shouts of "Oh my god, oh my god! What just happened?" as a voice confirms, "Alejandro and Wolf are not moving." A brief moment of hope arises as a voice exclaims, "Oh, he's alive!" referring to Wolf, who apparently went "all the way around," before urgent shouts of "Get in the car! Get in the car!" conclude the segment.

The segment opens with a close-up of a purple 'Fines' screen, displaying a $53 penalty for one casualty and zero bodies recovered, a cost echoed by a player's voice stating, "We lost $53." The scene transitions to a first-person perspective within a dimly lit, industrial corridor, filled with mechanical whirring and clanking. The player, in an orange suit with a black visor, holds a walkie-talkie and confidently declares, "I gave the shovel a test swing and you know what? I feel pretty confident with our chances," after testing a yellow-handled shovel. Another player asks if the shovel works on others, followed by a distinct impact sound and the first player confirming, "It does." As they navigate metal walkways with yellow railings and grated floors, the player suddenly exclaims, "What is that? Oh my god! There's a fucking portal turret over there, you guys!" accompanied by rapid gunfire and laser sounds. The player then confirms, "It's gone. It's dead." They discover deceased teammates, 'Alejandro' and 'savannahXYZ,' lying on the ground, but are hindered from looting by a 'HANDS FULL' status due to a large axle they've picked up. Another player asks if they can at least get a shovel from the bodies. Moving forward, the player spots a yellow-and-black striped apparatus suspended above, reacting with an alarmed, "Oh! What's that?" Amidst red warning lights and industrial decay, the player drops a body, shouting, "I'm dropping Yasha, I think," and then, in a panic, cries out, "Oh my god! My bees! My bees! Oh my god! We lost so much!" as heavy breathing and a final mechanical whirring underscore the escalating danger. The HUD displays health, weight, and communication controls, while on-screen text confirms the 'LIFE SUPPORT: OFFLINE' status, leaving the player overwhelmed and running through the corridor as the segment concludes.

The scene opens from a first-person perspective, showing a character in an orange spacesuit with a black visor navigating a dimly lit, industrial control room. The character, speaking with a distorted, panicked voice, exclaims "What? Oh, damn it!" and expresses distress over losing 'bees' and heavy team losses, stating "We can't lose our bees" and "We've lost so much," echoed by a female teammate. The Team Avatars Interface confirms 'Scorpius (Dead)' and 'savannahXYZ (Dead)' in red. The player character questions, "Does he know?" if a teammate understands the situation, while another calm male voice initially claims to understand, then admits, "I don't understand actually." The player character frantically asks, "How do I use this? I never learned how to use this!" as the female teammate adds, "No, he doesn't have a license!" The player character then asks, "Do you think the shovel works on people?" to which the calm male voice replies, "We already tested that," and the female teammate questions, "I don't know, did we?" As the character moves through a corridor, a large yellow-and-black striped apparatus causes alarm, prompting the player to shout, "Oh my god!" and "Get out!" A distorted radio voice identifies as 'John Gamer,' warning, "the facility is not safe," which another male voice immediately contradicts, stating, "The facility is as safe as it's ever been," before John Gamer advises, "Don't listen to her." A sharp metallic clang is heard, followed by the player character's horrified cries of "No! Oh, they dragged them away!" and "No!" The character descends a metal staircase into a darker, hazardous industrial corridor, discovering the bodies of deceased teammates, including 'Alejandro' and 'savannahXYZ'. The player character reacts with profound shock, exclaiming "MY BOY!" and vowing, "I'll avenge you, brothers, the last thing I do!" The HUD displays 'HANDS FULL' after picking up a large axle, and the sequence ends with the character looting a body, holding a shovel, amidst red warning lights, emphasizing the grim atmosphere and mission failure.

The segment opens with a first-person perspective of a character in an orange spacesuit with a black visor, navigating a dimly lit industrial corridor with grated floors and yellow railings, accompanied by a high-pitched, distorted squeal and guttural sounds of struggle. The HUD displays health, weight, and flashlight controls as the character moves cautiously, flashlight in hand. A calm male voice states, "Facility's still not clear," followed by another male voice remarking on someone being "lonely" and "held up," before a third, authoritative voice instructs, "You stay here." As the character proceeds, a male voice asks, "I'm just looking for a little girl is here," prompting confused inquiries of "What little girl?" from two other voices, which is then punctuated by a sudden, loud, distorted scream. The scene shifts to a third-person view, showing the character being spectated as 'Wolf' and later 'savannahXYZ', both marked as deceased, while a male voice asks, "Are you in there, still, Wolf?" and then grimly confirms, "Wolf has something killing him actively," and later, "I think he's definitely dead." The player character continues through the dark, claustrophobic corridor, passing the bodies of fallen teammates amidst flickering lights and ominous red warnings. Upon entering a control room, multiple monitors display a 'PROFIT QUOTA: 10 / 1253' and 'DEADLINE: 2 Days', alongside planetary data warning of 'FAUNA: Dangerous creatures'. A male voice sighs, "Hey guys, this is good," but is interrupted by a female voice exclaiming, "You're wasting our inhaler! Dude, I have asthma, I need that!" to which a male voice replies, "I only need one hit. If I did two, that would be a problem." Another sudden, sharp "No!" and gurgling sound briefly punctuate the tension. The player then discovers a yellow 'Rubber ducky' on the floor, picking it up as an excited male voice declares, "Let's go! Look what I found!" and exclaims, "Whoa, yeah! A collectible!" The item's value (77) is displayed, and another female voice comments, "That's cool. I like your Funko Pop," as the player stands in the tense control room, holding the flashlight and their new, quirky find.

The minute opens in a dimly lit, industrial control room with metallic walls, control panels, and bunk beds, where a group of characters in orange spacesuits are gathered. A male voice repeatedly says "No, no, no, no, no" as a scraping sound is heard, followed by an exclamation, "Proving excuse!" Another male voice explains, "It's my invention to support friend Tom," adding that "He's gonna be supporting us for the rest of the journey." A female voice questions, "Are we not gonna sell him or?" to which the male voice firmly replies, "No, he's part of the team. And he's gonna stay." Another male voice then discusses, "This is anything like my baby orc, uh, he's gonna sell that thing, but it's done." As upbeat, retro-futuristic music begins, the first-person perspective shows the player, in an orange suit with a black visor, holding a yellow flashlight and a walkie-talkie. The player communicates, "Uh, Breaker, this is Fixer," with the on-screen text confirming the call. A distressed female voice, likely from the character with the pink visor who is seen seated and looking troubled, responds, "Fixer, I'm broken, over." A loud impact or explosion sound follows. The player, "Fixer," then states, "I could take her." Muffled voices are heard, with one male voice remarking, "It could be worse." An authoritative male voice suggests, "Okay, let's split up now." The scene shifts as the player moves through a dark, narrow industrial corridor with grated floors and pipes, using the flashlight to navigate. The environment becomes increasingly hazardous, with red warning lights and a 'WARNING INCREASED LEVELS OF RADIATION 70 mSv' overlay appearing. The player mentions, "Uh, yeah, Savannah, I guess," as deceased teammates 'Alejandro' and 'Wolf' are encountered lying on the ground, marked with red text and avatars, implying 'Savannah' is also a teammate. A female voice, possibly interacting with the player, dismissively says, "No, no, no," then adds, "I've had enough of you and your shovel. I'm team Yasha this game." The player or another male voice questions, "Wait, where's Yasha?" and "Yasha?" Another male voice pleads, "The mental safe for all of us, please," followed by gasps. A female voice asks for direction, "Are we going back in here or the fire exit?" Two male voices emphatically respond, "Fire exit!" One male voice then informs, "Um, we have a bracket, by the way," to which a female voice replies, "Wait, we do?" The male voice explains, "Yeah, 'cause we have a battery." A loud, mechanical whirring/rumbling sound fills the air as the player interacts with a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus, causing alarm. A male voice reassures, "Nobody's gonna be down," then questions, "What's the worst thing that could get us?" This is immediately followed by another loud impact/explosion and a startled "Oh!" The sequence ends with the player approaching a door labeled 'Scorpius,' with the walkie-talkie displaying 'Drop Walkie-talkie : [G]' and 'Send voice : [LMB],' indicating ongoing communication and mission progression amidst the escalating danger.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins in a dark, industrial corridor from a first-person perspective, with the player, clad in an orange suit and black visor, navigating using a flashlight, their HUD displaying vital statistics. A teammate exclaims, "Oh, Scorpius!" as the player encounters a large, grotesque creature on the ceiling, triggering panicked shouts of "Bug! I thought on the ceiling!" and "Get the fuck back in!" Following a brief screen blackout indicating "LIFE SUPPORT: OFFLINE," the perspective shifts to observing 'Alejandro' in a control room filled with monitors displaying "MONITORING: Scorpius," cluttered equipment, a yellow rubber ducky, and a walkie-talkie. The player moves through this control room, passing a large yellow-and-black striped apparatus, amidst shouts of "Come on, man!" and "Hey, bitch!" culminating in a frantic "What the hell happened?!" A commercial for 'Twirla' interrupts, featuring a calm female voice discussing commitments and introducing "The Twirla patch." Returning to the game, the player continues exploring the control room, interacting with consoles and noting the "Monitoring Screen Interface" showing "PROFIT QUOTA: 10 / 1253" and "DEADLINE: 2 Days." Red text indicating deceased teammates 'Alejandro (Dead)' and 'Wolf (Dead)' underscores the tense atmosphere, prompting a sarcastic comment, "Oh, look, you died together. That's really cute," met with the response, "That's not cute." The team plans to "go back and recover the items that we are missing," before a player states, "before we do this, I gotta..." The segment opens in a dimly lit, industrial control room where the player, in an orange spacesuit, uses a flashlight and takes a hit from a 'Tetraethylpurine Inhalant,' stating, 'So I'm using my inhaler.' The player communicates via walkie-talkie with 'Savannah,' asking, 'Hey Savannah, I can hear you back,' who inquires about the inhalant's contents. After the player announces, 'I'm gonna go ahead, I'll see you guys in there,' and collects a yellow rubber ducky, they move into a narrow, dark corridor. Savannah's voice exclaims, 'I think it's empty, you fuck!' followed by the player's frustrated reply, 'No, no, I can't hear you, I can't hear you!' As the player navigates the claustrophobic space, HUD updates show 'Scorpius,' 'Wolf,' and 'Alejandro' joining the ship. Savannah's voice expresses, 'This place is huge down here, I need a buddy,' and later asks about a 'red dot' on the wall. The atmosphere intensifies with frantic shouts, metallic clanging, and impact sounds, as a male voice yells, 'No, get back, you son of a bitch!' The player discovers the deceased body of 'savannahXYZ,' marked 'Dead,' and acquires a yellow shovel from it, while a 'Life Support: Offline' warning appears. The player then encounters a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus, the apparent source of the commotion. The scene concludes with the player spectating 'Scorpius' in the same corridor, as a male voice confirms, 'She's already dead!' and another expresses relief, 'I'm just so lucky I had the shovel on me.' Eerie, low background music begins, and the team discusses strange sounds, with one person identifying them as 'Minecraft cave sound,' and another confirming, 'It actually was,' before a final, unsettling noise.

The tense atmosphere is underscored by red text indicating deceased teammates 'Alejandro (Dead)' and 'Wolf (Dead)', prompting a sarcastic comment, "Oh, look, you died together. That's really cute," to which another voice responds, "That's not cute." The conversation continues with a plan to "go back and recover the items that we are missing," before someone states, "before we do this, I gotta..."

The segment opens in a dimly lit, industrial control room where the player, in an orange spacesuit, uses a flashlight and is heard taking 'one more hit' from a 'Tetraethylpurine Inhalant', stating, 'So I'm using my inhaler.' The player communicates via walkie-talkie with a female teammate, 'Savannah,' asking 'Hey Savannah, I can hear you back,' who then inquires about the inhalant's contents. After the player announces, 'I'm gonna go ahead, I'll see you guys in there,' and collects a yellow rubber ducky, they move into a narrow, dark corridor. Savannah's voice is heard exclaiming, 'I think it's empty, you fuck!' followed by the player's frustrated reply, 'No, no, I can't hear you, I can't hear you!' As the player navigates the claustrophobic space, HUD updates show 'Scorpius,' 'Wolf,' and 'Alejandro' joining the ship. Savannah's voice expresses, 'This place is huge down here, I need a buddy,' and later asks about a 'red dot' seen on the wall. The atmosphere intensifies with frantic shouts, metallic clanging, and impact sounds, as a male voice yells, 'No, get back, you son of a bitch!' The player discovers the deceased body of 'savannahXYZ,' marked 'Dead,' and acquires a yellow shovel from it, while a 'Life Support: Offline' warning appears. The player then encounters a large, yellow-and-black striped apparatus, which seems to be the source of the commotion. The scene concludes with the player spectating 'Scorpius' in the same foreboding corridor, as a male voice confirms, 'She's already dead!' and another expresses relief, 'I'm just so lucky I had the shovel on me.' Eerie, low background music begins, and the team discusses strange sounds, with one person identifying them as 'Minecraft cave sound,' and another confirming, 'It actually was,' before a final, unsettling noise.

event_blocks.summary: The Character in orange suit with black visor navigates a dimly lit industrial corridor, the atmosphere underscored by a low hum and metallic sounds, reassuringly stating, "I'll be here, buddy, don't worry, I got your back." The character encounters the Deceased Teammate Bodies of 'savannahXYZ' and 'Alejandro', prompting the Character in orange suit with blue visor to remark, "Well, well, well, guess it's just the three of us, bozos." Panic erupts with the Character in orange suit with green visor exclaiming, "Is that? running the fuck away!", followed by interjections from the Character in orange suit with pink visor. A shift to a recording interface reveals a hand holding a purple object with a smiley face, as voices discuss camera film before a commercial-like voice thanks viewers and promotes liking and subscribing, accompanied by a whimsical electronic jingle. The scene transitions to a brightly lit Debriefing Room where characters in orange suits with pink, blue, and green visors, some holding Name Tags like 'man' and 'uwu', appear distressed on a couch. A large screen displays a SPOOK TUBE Interface. A female voice inquires about the mission's success, to which the Character in orange suit with pink visor expresses a desire to distance themselves from the content. The Character in orange suit with blue visor asks 'Tom', identified as the Yellow Rubber Ducky with Name Tag, for his opinion, eliciting a desperate plea, "Don't post this, please! Please!" The Character in orange suit with black visor concludes with, "I think that not saying anything is the best compliment sometimes. Like right now," and the Character in orange suit with green visor adds, "All right, yeah. You know what? Thanks, guys."
